title: US Telephone Directory OCR Corpus: A Machine-Readable Panel of Mid-Century American Yellow Pages (1950-1978)
description:
A machine-readable corpus of 168 historical Yellow Pages phonebooks digitized from the Library of Congress US Telephone Directories collection (public domain), spanning five states, 44 cities, and the years 1950-1978. The corpus contains approximately 2.1 million business listings with page locations and Yellow Pages category headings; a richer merged layer of 110 books adds word-level bounding boxes and automated category-header detection (~3.7 million tokens, 17,995 categories). Also included are 510 hand-transcribed merchant-year records of bank credit card acceptance drawn from the directories' dedicated 'Credit Card' sections.

Released as the public-data companion to 'Credit Cards and Retail Firms: Historical Evidence from the United States' (Joseph P. Hall). The proprietary Dun & Bradstreet establishment microdata used in the paper is NOT included and must be licensed separately. See the included data dictionary for schema and provenance.
notes: Source scans are public domain (Library of Congress). OCR processing by Hi-Tech Inc. Corpus compilation and analysis code by Joseph P. Hall, released under CC-BY-4.0.
